sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system data documented in available sources

special_mechanics.name: dash
special_mechanics.description: Speed dashes available as part of character move set

physics_values.note: Mentioned in gameplay description but no specific physics values provided

special_mechanics.name: character_differentiation
special_mechanics.description: Three playable androids with different attributes: Ninja (slow, powerful), Kamaitachi (quick, weak), Kunoichi (balanced)

special_mechanics.name: grab_and_throw
special_mechanics.description: Can grab and throw environmental objects like motorcycles and safes at enemies

tier_notes: Tier 2 - Empirical/qualitative data only. Sources describe gameplay mechanics and character differentiation but contain no quantified physics parameters (speeds, acceleration, gravity values). Movement is noted as 'tight' and 'responsive' by reviewers. Game features three characters with explicitly different speed/strength attributes but exact numerical values are not documented. Side-scrolling beat 'em up with linear stage progression.
sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor (gameplay mechanics, character descriptions, controls assessment)
wikipedia - The Ninja Warriors (1994 video game) (gameplay description, character attributes, critical reception mentioning tight/responsive controls)
